Singapore.title: Reform Party leader Kenneth Jeyaretnam dies aged 67
Singapore.snippet: [SINGAPORE] Kenneth Jeyaretnam, secretary-general of the Reform Party, died on July 18. He was 67.

Singapore.title: Reform Party leader Kenneth Jeyaretnam dies at 67
Singapore.snippet: He died peacefully in his sleep surrounded by family, his wife said.

United States.title: Iran War Live Updates: U.S. Launches Strikes After Iranian Attack Kills 2 American Soldiers
United States.snippet: The soldiers, killed in Jordan, were the first U.S. troops to die from hostile fire since an April truce. U.S. forces began retaliatory strikes early Sunday.

United States.title: In Maine, Troy Jackson Gains Momentum in Bid to Replace Platner
United States.snippet: Troy Jackson, a progressive former State Senate president, showed signs of significant strength, as half the state’s counties picked delegates to a convention where they will choose Graham Platner’s replacement.

United States.title: Vance Remarks on Epstein and Israel Echo Conspiracy Theories
United States.snippet: The vice president’s comment that there were ties between Mr. Epstein and the “highest levels” of Israeli intelligence troubled some in his party.

United States.title: Adidas Hopes Its World Cup Moment Can Drive U.S. Sales
United States.snippet: Nearly everything on the field on Sunday will bear the German company’s logo. Bjørn Gulden, the chief executive, says he ‘couldn’t have scripted it better.’

Southeast Asia (SEA).title: Woman dies week after Bangkok bar fire, raising toll to 34
Southeast Asia (SEA).snippet: The 20-year-old woman had died in hospital early on Sunday (Jul 19), officials from Erawan Emergency Medical Centre said, adding that 13 still remain in intensive care.

Southeast Asia (SEA).title: Roads partially reopen at Bangkok tunnel leak site
Southeast Asia (SEA).snippet: Roads were partially reopened to traffic near Bangkok's Wongwian Yai on Sunday as repairs continued underground to the leaking Purple Line rail tunnel in Klong San district, which earlier caused a subsidence.

Southeast Asia (SEA).title: Corrupt officials blamed for businesses illegally run by foreigners
Southeast Asia (SEA).snippet: Corrupt government officials were viewed as the main factor allowing foreigners to illegally operate businesses in Thailand, a Nida poll showed on Sunday.

Southeast Asia (SEA).title: Rescuers find five survivors and are still looking for 20 others after boat sinks in Indonesia
Southeast Asia (SEA).snippet: The five, including a 7-year-old girl, were found clinging to a fish trap belonging to a local fisherman, Makassar search and rescue officials said.

World Affairs.title: England survive France fightback to secure third place in Miami goalfest
World Affairs.snippet: MIAMI: England held off a stunning France fightback to claim a 6-4 victory in a madcap Miami thriller on Saturday, securing third place for their best World Cup finish since their sole title triumph in 1966.

World Affairs.title: Why Tokyo’s New Shorts Policy is Sparking Debate in Japan
World Affairs.snippet: The Tokyo government is allowing workers to wear shorts for the first time this summer, prompting delicate questions about etiquette and hairy legs.

World Affairs.title: Ukraine’s Top General Becomes a Target of Protesters’ Anger
World Affairs.snippet: Demonstrators supporting Ukraine’s ousted defense minister have begun denouncing the commander he clashed with in office, Gen. Oleksandr Syrskyi.

World Affairs.title: Cuban dissident Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara goes into exile in US
World Affairs.snippet: He was arrested in 2021 during Cuba's largest anti-government protests in decades and spent five years ⁠in prison.

Geopolitics.title: US and Iran exchange strikes after two US soldiers killed in Jordan
Geopolitics.snippet: US forces target Iranian sites for the eighth night in a row, while Iran says it fired drones at the US military in Kuwait.

Geopolitics.title: Madison Square Garden Sues Wired Magazine Over L.G.B.T.Q. Tracking Report
Geopolitics.snippet: The Manhattan venue claims a July article from the technology magazine falsely implied that it tracked information about some celebrities’ sexual orientation for “discriminatory purposes.”

Geopolitics.title: Opinion: Beware the unintended consequences of testosterone screening for military servicemembers
Geopolitics.snippet: “I fear that the widespread rollout of testosterone screening may lead to some surprising and unintended consequences,” writes a urologist who studies testosterone.

Geopolitics.title: The Rise and Fall of Ukraine’s Drone Warfare Mastermind
Geopolitics.snippet: The popularity of drones, and the defense minister who heavily promoted them, helped lead to a clash in which President Volodymyr Zelensky sided with the military brass.

Healthcare & Biotech.title: Tate Brothers Arrested in Miami As U.K. Authorities File Sex Crime Charges
Healthcare & Biotech.snippet: Federal marshals arrested the influencers, who are known for their misogynistic views and have faced years of criminal investigations in Europe. The new charges concern offenses dating to 2010, British officials said.

Healthcare & Biotech.title: Walt Odets, Who Delved Into the Minds of Gay Men, Dies at 79
Healthcare & Biotech.snippet: A psychologist, he wrote during the AIDS crisis about survivor’s guilt and depression in his H.I.V.-negative patients.

Healthcare & Biotech.title: Wife of Indian activist Wangchuk asks court to move him to private hospital
Healthcare & Biotech.snippet: “Despite repeated requests, the hospital has refused to discharge him or allow us to shift him to a private hospital of our choice,” Gitanjali Angmo said in a post on X on Sunday (Jul 19).

Healthcare & Biotech.title: These AIPAC-Backed Democrats Voted to Nix Israel Aid. AIPAC Punished Them.
Healthcare & Biotech.snippet: Of the 103 Democrats who voted to eliminate aid to Israel, more than a dozen had been endorsed by the pro-Israel lobbying group. AIPAC quickly closed off online donations for them.

Technology & AI.title: Prompt Injection Attacks Are Thwarting AI Hacking Agents
Technology & AI.snippet: “Context bombing” tricks malicious AI agents into shutting down before they can do harm.

Technology & AI.snippet: This isn’t the first time power outages have caused issues for Waymo.

Technology & AI.title: Kimi: Threat or menace?
Technology & AI.snippet: Chinese company Moonshot AI released a new version of its Kimi model this week, prompting concern about "full AI communism."

Technology & AI.title: Will AI fix prior authorization—or make it worse?
Technology & AI.snippet: The government is piloting a program that uses AI for insurance-coverage decisions.

Business & Finance.title: Russians turn to cash, putting more strain on slowing wartime economy
Business & Finance.snippet: Russians have been hit by mobile internet shutdowns and more businesses are seeking to dodge tax after more than four years of war with Ukraine.

Business & Finance.title: World Cup Final Drives Billions in Bets on Kalshi and Polymarket
Business & Finance.snippet: The soccer tournament has helped propel the prediction markets to new user records, creating what may be the largest gambling event in history.

Business & Finance.title: ‘They are relentless’: A scammer is trying to change the email on my empty Robinhood account. What do they have to gain?
Netflix is spending big money on sports. Is the company making the right bets?
Business & Finance.snippet: The streaming service says live programming helps draw in new subscribers, but investors have become disillusioned with Netflix’s broader engagement trends.
